rows.notes: Top line reads clearly as a carried‑over balance. Pounds, shillings and pence digits are well aligned in the rightmost columns and pass currency validation. Handwriting of 'Soluta Varia' is legible enough to transcribe; minor flourish on 'Bro't' but amount is unambiguous.
Clear structural label with no monetary amounts. Text is legible and clearly introduces the list below.

Amount columns (53 6 0) are clear and align with the vertical ruling. The name/first word is slightly ambiguous (looks like 'Stile' or possibly 'File'/'Hile'); chosen reading 'Stile' based on letter-forms and similarity to surrounding capitals.
Description and occupation clearly legible. Amount (17 2 0) is crisply written and aligns exactly with the pounds/shillings/pence columns; values within valid ranges.
Name and occupation clearly read as 'Hayes - Bookbinder'. Amount 16s 1s 6d (16 1 6) is well aligned with ledger columns and unambiguous.
Clear reading: 'Vincent - Printer'. Amount 8 pounds 18 shillings 0 pence is crisply written and in the correct columns; shillings value (18) is within 0–19.
'Baxter - Do' (ditto) is legible; amounts 11 10 0 are clearly aligned. Minor flourish on the 'Do' but no ambiguity in numbers.

Amount (6 6 6) is clearly written and in correct columns. The name looks like 'Briens' (could be alternate spelling/hand) and 'Stationary &c.' is inferred from the script; moderate uncertainty on exact name spelling but numbers are unambiguous.
Clear reading of name and occupation. Amount 3 3 0 is neatly aligned; no ambiguity in digits.
Name and occupation are clearly legible. Amount 45 7 0 is crisp and aligns with the ledger ruling lines; values valid.

Name transcribed as common abbreviation 'Wm Bettell' (handwritten abbreviation visible). Amount reads as 21 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence with a small loop after the pence digit interpreted as the halfpenny symbol ('ob') → 0.5. The fraction mark is slightly faint but consistent with a halfpenny; numbers otherwise align well.

Boxed final total at bottom-right with pounds/shillings/pence and a halfpenny (0.5) loop. Total matches the sum of the carried-over amount plus the listed tradesmen entries (verified by converting to pence and summing). The boxed digits are a little stylized but align to the pounds/shillings/pence columns; shillings (6) is clearly within 0–19 and pence 9 with halfpenny is consistent.
